mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive forward returns when both series are in the top quartile, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when FEDFUNDS changes by more than 0.25% in the preceding month, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of rising INDPRO observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in >50% of cases when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer confidence and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion and corporate profit growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 shows positive growth, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.03 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals weakening manufacturing activity and impending corporate earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >50% of instances where INDPRO drops >0.5%, or the threshold signal fails to achieve statistical significance with p > 0.05 in out-of-sample classification tests.
